title: Decoder output-projection producer memory hierarchy
hypothesis: After policy-wrapper ranker calibration, output projection remains dominant because producer weight delivery is modeled as streaming. Sweeping cache capacity, cache hit rate, local-cache bandwidth, off-chip bandwidth, and compute lanes will identify whether a resident/sharded weight hierarchy can move the frontier.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How much producer output-projection latency can be removed by plausible weight-cache capacity and bandwidth before compute becomes the limiter?
include: cache capacity and effective hit-rate sweep
serial and parallel cache/miss service bounds

expected_benefit: separates producer compute parallelism from weight-memory hierarchy requirements
estimates the cache capacity and local bandwidth needed to reduce the current 301-401 us producer bottleneck
selects whether the next measured RTL job should be a resident/sharded weight-cache producer or a different output-projection mapping
risks: cache hit rate is an architectural assumption, not measured reuse from a concrete scheduler
parallel cache/off-chip service is optimistic when the local cache and refill path share ports
large resident weight capacity may be impractical on Nangate45 without a macro/SRAM model

required_evaluations.objective: Estimate output-projection producer latency under explicit weight-memory hierarchy assumptions after calibrated ranker service.

expected_result.reason: If moderate cache capacity/bandwidth removes the producer bottleneck, queue a measured resident/sharded producer; otherwise revisit output-projection mapping or external memory assumptions.
